messages.content: what does it mean to say that memories are “context dependent” A. people remember information best when they’re in the original place where they learned it. B. people remember information in up to seven locations C. people often attach their surroundings to their memories D. both a and b
D. both a and b

how do emotions affect your memory? A. memories with strong emotional attachments are easier to recall than other memories B. emotions stunt your ability to remember your sensitive thoughts C. emotions have a neutral impact and don't affect your memory D. none of the above
A. memories with strong emotional attachments are easier to recall than other memories
lyndon johnson served as president _ vice-president.  A. truman B. nixon C. eisenhower D. kennedy
D. Kennedy

select multiple answers. which of the following represent president johnsons continued support of the kennedy administration? A. reliance on kennedy staff B. u.s. commitment to south vietnam C. establishment of the great society D. civil rights legislation

A. reliance on kennedy staff
B. U.S. commitment to South Vietnam

select multiple answers. which of the following are early actions the johnson administration took regarding vietnam? A. drafted a resolution of congressional support for u.s. military action in vietnam B. stepped up aid to north vietnam C. sent two divisions of combat troops to south vietnam D. increased the number of american military advisors in south vietnam

A. drafted a resolution of congressional support for U.S. military action in Vietnam
D. increased the number of American military advisors in South Vietnam
the communist-backed guerrillas in south vietnam _ influence after the overthrow of ngo dinh diem. A. gained B. lost
A. gained

president johnson believed that _ A. it would be a betrayal of the united states south vietnamese allies if the united states was to pull out vietnam B. the number of american military advisors in south vietnam should be reduced C. the united states should pull out of vietnam D. president kennedy had mismanaged u.s. foreign policy in vietnam
A. it would be a betrayal of the United States' South Vietnamese allies if the United States was to pull out of Vietnam

what was the great society? A. robert mcnamaras study on u.s. foreign policy B. president johnsons domestic reform program C. president johnsons policy for the vietnam war D. president kennedys civil rights bill
B. President Johnson's domestic reform program
what was OPLAN 34A? A. a north vietnamese intelligence unit B. a u.s. sponspored program of secret raids on north vietnam C. an american warship D. a south vietnamese tropedo boat
B. a U.S. sponsored program of secret raids on North Vietnam

select multiple answers. which of the following events occured before and during the gulf of tonkin incident on august 4? A. the maddox and turner joy fired into the dark in the direction of what they thought were torpedo boats B. president johnson announced to the american people that the united states has been supporting convert raids on north Vietnam C. the maddox and turner joy reported objects approaching them in the water D. james stockdale spotted north vietnamese torpedo boat approaching the turner joy

A. The Maddox and Turner Joy fired into the dark in the direction of what they thought were torpedo boats
C. The Maddox and Turner Joy reported objects approaching them in the water
D. James Stockdale spotted a North Vietnamese torpedo boat approaching the Turner Joy
true or false, the U.S.S. maddox suffered heavy losses during the August 2 attack by North Vietnamese torpedo boats
False. The USS Maddox did not suffer heavy losses during the August 2 attack by North Vietnamese torpedo boats. In fact, there were no US casualties reported in that incident.

what was the U.S.S. Maddox doing in the Gulf of tonkin prior to the august 2 attack? A. she was headed for china's costline B. she was retireving wreckage from the U.S.S. turner joy C. she was on a routine patrol D. she was on an intelligence mission to intercept north vietnamese communications
C. She was on a routine patrol.
president johnson ordered air strikes against north vietnam following the _ attack on american destroyers in the gulf of tonkin. A. first B. second C. third
B. second
Rhetoric can be consdiered _ A. a science B. a kind of grammar C. an argument that always works D. an art
D. an art
